get.summary: Get the MyForterro API consumption report of a tenant.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: ID of the tenant.

parameters.name: From
parameters.description: Date and time to start reporting consumption from. ISO-8601 format.

parameters.name: Until
parameters.description: Date and time to end reporting consumption at. ISO-8601 format.

200.description: Consumption report of the specified tenant.

400.description: The request is invalid. Check the response body for more details.

500.description: An unexpected error occurred. Check the response body for more details.

403.description: You are not authorized to perform this action.

tenantId.description: ID of the tenant this invoicing configuration applies to.

maxDailyTokens.description:
Maximum number of tokens the tenant can use daily. -1 means no limit.
Takes precedence over the global limit if set.

openAIApiKey.description: The OpenAI API key for the tenant. If not set, the global API key will be used.

tenantId.description: ID of the tenant owning this bill.

receivedAt.description: UTC date and time when the bill was received. ISO-8601 format.

processedAt.description:
UTC date and time when the bill processing was finished. ISO-8601 format.
This field is null if the bill is still being processed.

errors.description: Should the bill processing status be failed, this field will contain the list of errors causing the failure.

validationErrors.description: Should the bill validation and conversion fail, this field will contain the list of errors causing the failure.

isAcknowledged.description: Indicate if the bill has been marked as acknowledged.

fromEmail.description: Should the bill be received by email, this field will contain the email address of the sender.

BillReceptionMode.description: Mode used to receive the bill.

like.description: User feedback: true for like, false for dislike, null to remove feedback.

messageId.description:
ID of the message to overwrite with this chat request.
When specified, the target message content will be replaced with this request and every following messages in the conversation will be purged
and replaced with the new responses.

maxDailyTokens.description: The maximum number of tokens that can be used per day. If -1, there is no limit.

openAIApiKey.description: The OpenAI API key for the tenant. If not set, the global API key will be used.

description.description: Optional description of the agent's purpose.

model.description: AI model to use for this agent.

instructions.description: Instruction that defines the agent's behavior.

temperature.description: Temperature parameter for model sampling (0.0 to 2.0).

maxTokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate.

mcpServers.description: List of MCP servers the agent can use.

apiKeyId.description: Esker API Key Identifier. Required when invoicing system is Esker and ignored otherwise.

apiKey.description: Esker API Key. Required when invoicing system is Esker and ignored otherwise.

EditInvoicingConfigurationDto.description: Configure the MyForterro API invoicing module for a tenant.

slug.description:
Case-insensitive name of the tenant.
This name should be unique and only contain US-ASCII characters. As it will be used in URLs, it will be
stored in lowercase and any non-URL encoded characters that should have been encoded will cause it to be
rejected.

applicationId.description:
ID of the MyForterro application associated with the tenant.
This information is not required for a tenant to exist, but it won't be able to use the Invoicing service
until the information has been provided.

myForterroClientId.description:
Client ID of the MyForterro application associated with the tenant.
This information is not required for a tenant to exist, but it won't be able to use the MyForterroAPI services
until the information has been provided.

myForterroClientSecret.description:
Client secret of the MyForterro application associated with the tenant.
This information is not required for a tenant to exist, but it won't be able to use the MyForterroAPI services
until the information has been provided.

EditTenantDto.description:
Tenant edition request.
A tenant can only start using the MyForterroAPI services after it has been fully configured.

base.description: Base currency of the rate query.

FxResultDto.description: Result of a foreign exchange rate query.

tenantId.description: ID of the tenant owning this invoice.

emittedAt.description: UTC date and time when the invoice was emitted. ISO-8601 format.

processedAt.description:
UTC date and time when the invoice processing was finished. ISO-8601 format.
This field is null if the invoice is still being processed.

errors.description: Should the invoice processing status be failed, this field will contain the list of errors causing the failure.

validationErrors.description: Should the invoice validation and conversion fail, this field will contain the list of errors causing the failure.

isAcknowledged.description: Indicate if the invoice has been marked as acknowledged.

receivedBillCount.description: Number of bills received by the tenant.

transmittedInvoiceCount.description: Number of invoices transmitted by the tenant.

totalConsumption.description: Sum of all received bills and transmitted invoices.

InvoicingSystemType.description: Type of invoicing system configured for a tenant.

headers.description: A dictionary of header to pass to the MCP when it is called

MyForterroTenantIdentifiersWithSecretDto.description: MyForterro identifiers of a tenant, including its client secret.

NationalPlatformStatus.description: Possible business statuses of a bill received via a national platform.

type.description: The type of the URL citation. Always url_citation.

OpenAIAnnotationDto.description: Annotations for the message, when applicable, as when using the web search tool.

content.description:
The contents of the assistant message. Required unless tool_calls or function_call is specified.
Text or array of content parts.

name.description: An optional name for the participant. Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

tool_calls.description: The tool calls generated by the model, such as function calls.

refusal.description: The refusal message by the assistant.

id.description: Unique identifier for this audio response.

expires_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when this audio response will no longer be accessible on the server for use in multi-turn conversations.

data.description: Base64 encoded audio bytes generated by the model, in the format specified in the request.

transcript.description: Transcript of the audio generated by the model.

OpenAIAudioDto.description: Parameters for audio output. Required when audio output is requested with modalities: ["audio"].

index.description: The index of the choice in the list of choices.

refusal.description: The refusal message generated by the model.

tool_calls.description: The tool calls generated by the model, such as function calls.

OpenAIChatCompletionDeltaDto.description: Represents a chat completion delta generated by the model in an OpenAI response.

choices.description: A list of chat completion choices. Can be more than one if n is greater than 1.

created.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when the chat completion was created.

id.description: A unique identifier for the chat completion.

model.description: The model used for the chat completion.

system_fingerprint.description:
This fingerprint represents the backend configuration that the model runs with.
Can be used in conjunction with the seed request parameter to understand when backend changes have been made that might impact determinism.

refusal.description: The refusal message generated by the model.

annotations.description: Annotations for the message, when applicable, as when using the web search tool.

tool_calls.description: The tool calls generated by the model, such as function calls.

OpenAIChatCompletionMessageDto.description:
Represents a chat completion message generated by the model in an OpenAI response.
This is different from request messages as it includes additional fields like tool_calls, refusal, etc.

messages.description:
A list of messages comprising the conversation so far. Depending on the model you use, different message types (modalities) are supported, like text,
images, and audio.

model.description: Myforterro model ID used to generate the response

frequency_penalty.description:
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to
repeat the same line verbatim.

function_call.description:
Deprecated in favor of tool_choice.
Controls which (if any) function is called by the model.
`none` means the model will not call a function and instead generates a message.
`auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling a function.
Specifying a particular function via {"name": "my_function"} forces the model to call that function.
`none` is the default when no functions are present. auto is the default if functions are present.

functions.description:
Deprecated in favor of tools.
A list of functions the model may generate JSON inputs for.

logit_bias.description: Modify the likelihood of specified tokens appearing in the completion.

logprobs.description:
Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not.
If true, returns the log probabilities of each output token returned in the content of message.

max_completion_tokens.description: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a completion, including visible output tokens and reasoning tokens.

max_tokens.description:
Deprecated in favor of max_completion_tokens.
The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the chat completion. This value can be used to control costs for text generated via API.
This value is now deprecated in favor of max_completion_tokens, and is not compatible with o-series models.

metadata.description:
Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object.
This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format, and querying for objects via API or the dashboard.
Keys are strings with a maximum length of 64 characters. Values are strings with a maximum length of 512 characters.

modalities.description:
Output types that you would like the model to generate.
Most models are capable of generating text, which is the default:
["text"]
The gpt-4o-audio-preview model can also be used to generate audio.
To request that this model generate both text and audio responses, you can use:
["text", "audio"]

n.description:
How many chat completion choices to generate for each input message.
Note that you will be charged based on the number of generated tokens across all of the choices.
Keep n as 1 to minimize costs.

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to enable parallel function calling during tool use.

prediction.description:
Configuration for a Predicted Output, which can greatly improve response times when large parts of the model response are known ahead of time.
This is most common when you are regenerating a file with only minor changes to most of the content.

presence_penalty.description:
Number between -2.0 and 2.0.
Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

prompt_cache_key.description: Used to cache responses for similar requests to optimize your cache hit rates. Replaces the user field.

safety_identifier.description:
A stable identifier used to help detect users of your application that may be violating OpenAI's usage policies.
The IDs should be a string that uniquely identifies each user.
We recommend hashing their username or email address, in order to avoid sending us any identifying information.

seed.description:
Deprecated.
If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return
the same result.
Determinism is not guaranteed, and you should refer to the system_fingerprint response parameter to monitor changes in the backend.

stop.description:
Not supported with latest reasoning models.
Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens. The returned text will not contain the stop sequence.

store.description: Whether to store the chat completion or not.

stream.description: If set to true, the model response data will be streamed to the client as it is generated using server-sent events.

temperature.description:
What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2.
Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic.
We generally recommend altering this or top_p but not both.

tool_choice.description:
Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model.
`none` means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message.
`auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools.
`required` means the model must call one or more tools.
Specifying a particular tool via {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "my_function"}} forces the model to call that tool.
`none` is the default when no tools are present. auto is the default if tools are present.

tools.description: A list of tools the model may call. You can provide either custom tools or function tools.

top_logprobs.description:
An integer between 0 and 20 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability.
logprobs must be set to true if this parameter is used.

top_p.description:
An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass.
So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.
We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

user.description:
This field is being replaced by safety_identifier and prompt_cache_key. Use prompt_cache_key instead to maintain caching optimizations.
A stable identifier for your end-users.
Used to boost cache hit rates by better bucketing similar requests and to help OpenAI detect and prevent abuse.

web_search_options.description: This tool searches the web for relevant results to use in a response.

end_index.description: The index of the last character of the URL citation in the message.

start_index.description: The index of the first character of the URL citation in the message.

title.description: The title of the web resource.

url.description: The URL of the web resource.

audio_tokens.description: Audio input tokens generated by the model.

accepted_prediction_tokens.description: When using Predicted Outputs, the number of tokens in the prediction that appeared in the completion.

rejected_prediction_tokens.description:
When using Predicted Outputs, the number of tokens in the prediction that did not appear in the completion.
However, like reasoning tokens, these tokens are still counted in the total completion tokens for purposes of billing, output, and context window limits.

content.description: Can be string OR array of content parts (but only "text" content parts are valid)

name.description:
An optional name for the participant.
Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

OpenAIFinishReason.description:
The reason the model stopped generating tokens.
This will be stop if the model hit a natural stop point or a provided stop sequence,
length if the maximum number of tokens specified in the request was reached, content_filter if content was omitted due to a flag from our content filters,
tool_calls if the model called a tool,
or function_call (deprecated) if the model called a function.

arguments.description:
The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON format.
Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema.
Validate the arguments in your code before calling your function.

name.description: The name of the function to call.
The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

description.description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.

parameters.description:
The parameters the functions accepts, described as a JSON Schema object.
See the guide for examples, and the JSON Schema reference for documentation about the format.
Omitting parameters defines a function with an empty parameter list.

strict.description:
Whether to enable strict schema adherence when generating the function call.
If set to true, the model will follow the exact schema defined in the parameters field.
Only a subset of JSON Schema is supported when strict is true.

name.description: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

description.description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.

parameters.description: The parameters the functions accepts, described as a JSON Schema object.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

content.description: A list of message content tokens with log probability information.

id.description: Unique identifier for a previous audio response from the model.

OpenAIMessageAudioDto.description: Data about a previous audio response from the model.

cached_tokens.description: Cached tokens present in the prompt.

audio_tokens.description: Audio input tokens present in the prompt.

voice.description:
The voice the model uses to respond.
Supported voices are alloy, ash, ballad, coral, echo, fable, nova, onyx, sage, and shimmer.

format.description:
Specifies the output audio format.
For OpenAI provider, it must be one of wav, mp3, flac, opus, or pcm16.

OpenAIResponseFormatType.description:
An object specifying the format that the model must output.
Setting to { "type": "json_schema", "json_schema": {...} } enables Structured Outputs which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON schema.
Setting to { "type": "json_object" } enables the older JSON mode, which ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON.
Using json_schema is preferred for models that support it.

include_obfuscation.description:
When true, stream obfuscation will be enabled.
Stream obfuscation adds random characters to an obfuscation field on streaming delta events to normalize payload sizes as a mitigation to certain
side-channel attacks.
These obfuscation fields are included by default, but add a small amount of overhead to the data stream.
You can set include_obfuscation to false to optimize for bandwidth if you trust the network links between your application and the OpenAI API.

include_usage.description:
If set, an additional chunk will be streamed before the data: [DONE] message.
The usage field on this chunk shows the token usage statistics for the entire request, and the choices field will always be an empty array.
All other chunks will also include a usage field, but with a null value.
NOTE: If the stream is interrupted, you may not receive the final usage chunk which contains the total token usage for the request.

content.description: Can be string OR array of content parts (but only "text" content parts are valid)

name.description:
An optional name for the participant.
Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

logprob.description:
The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens.
Otherwise, the value -9999.0 is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

bytes.description:
A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token.
Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text
representation.
Can be null if there is no bytes representation for the token.

top_logprobs.description:
List of the most likely tokens and their log probability, at this token position.
In rare cases, there may be fewer than the number of requested top_logprobs returned.

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

type.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only function is supported.

content.description: Can be string OR array of content parts (but only "text" content parts are valid)

tool_call_id.description: Tool call that this message is responding to.

logprob.description:
The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens.
Otherwise, the value -9999.0 is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

bytes.description:
A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token.
Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text
representation.
Can be null if there is no bytes representation for the token.

completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the generated completion.

prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the prompt.

total_tokens.description: Total number of tokens used in the request (prompt + completion).

content.description:
The contents of the user message.
Can be string OR array of content parts (text, image_url, input_audio or file)

name.description: An optional name for the participant. Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

from.description: Date and time to start reporting consumption from. ISO-8601 format.

until.description: Date and time to end reporting consumption at. ISO-8601 format.

TenantConsumptionFiltersDto.description: Filters to apply to a tenant consumption report request.

tenantId.description: ID of the tenant this consumption report was generated for.

TenantConsumptionReportDto.description: MyForterro API consumption report of a tenant.

tenantId.description: ID of the tenant this invoicing configuration applies to.

eskerApiKeyId.description: Esker API Key Identifier when the invoicing system of the tenant is Esker.

TenantInvoicingConfigurationDto.description: Invoicing configuration of a MyForterro API tenant.

location.description: Location of the validation error in the invoice.

Bearer.description:
JWT Authorization header using the Bearer scheme. 

 
                      Enter 'Bearer' [space] and then your token in the text input below.
                      

Example: 'Bearer 12345abcdef'
Bearer.name: Authorization
